title: Johrenia alpina
intro: Johrenia alpina, also known as Alpine Joint-pine or Mountain Joint-pine, is a perennial plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems. Johrenia alpina contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Johrenia alpina include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions like bronchitis, arthritis, and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Johrenia alpina is classified within the family Apiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Johrenia, which is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Johrenia alpina is a type of perennial plant and is native to the mountainous regions of Southern Africa. It is further classified as a member of the tribe Scandiceae, which includes various species of flowering plants. The classification of Johrenia alpina provides valuable information about its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
